page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ae.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

The underlying AMD EPYC processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of a 32 KB L1 data cache, a 1.0 MB L2 cache, and a 32 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction set extensions, including AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, which accelerate vector and neural network operations. Cryptographic tasks are accelerated via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets, while integer operations benefit from BMI2, ADX, and FMA support.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.g8ae.4xlarge demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% of tested instances with a stress-ng single-core score of 3113.99. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 24929.84 and uncached read and write memory bandwidths of 48.45 GB/sec and 28.15 GB/sec respectively. Database operations and static web serving throughput also align with mid-range expectations, yielding 8023.11 operations on PassMark and 18.28 GB/sec in static web serving.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the instance offers stable performance without resource contention, representing a balanced cost-to-resource profile for general-purpose workloads. The absence of local storage and GPUs makes it less suitable for heavy storage-bound databases or deep learning training. Instead, it is well-suited for applications such as web serving, application servers, microservices, and moderate database workloads that benefit from strong single-core performance and standard memory density.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ae.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at 3.4 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps without local storage or GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, while its multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. Supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and SHA_NI, this instance provides a stable, dedicated resource profile. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, application hosting, and moderate database operations that require consistent CPU performance without the need for local NVMe storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ae.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance offering balanced compute and memory resources with top-tier single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9T34 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Supports AVX-512, vector, and cryptographic instruction set extensions
Designed for web serving, application hosting, and general-purpose workloads
